name: Ad Hominem
description: Attacking the character or circumstances of the person making an argument rather than the argument itself.

name: Straw Man
description: Misrepresenting an opponent's argument to make it easier to attack.

name: False Dilemma
description: Presenting only two options when more alternatives exist.
trigger_phrases: if we don't then we must

name: Appeal to Authority
description: Citing an authority figure as evidence without further support.

name: Slippery Slope
description: Claiming a small action will inevitably lead to extreme consequences without evidence.

name: Hasty Generalisation
description: Drawing broad conclusions from a small or unrepresentative sample.

name: Circular Reasoning
description: Using the conclusion as a premise in the argument.
trigger_phrases: that is why it must be
as we can see from the fact that

name: Appeal to Emotion
description: Manipulating emotional responses rather than using valid arguments.

name: Bandwagon (Appeal to Popularity)
description: Arguing something is correct because many people believe it.

name: Legal Misapplication
description: Citing a legal instrument outside its actual scope or misrepresenting its provisions.

name: False Equivalence
description: Treating two situations as equivalent when they have significant differences.

name: Post Hoc (False Causality)
description: Assuming that because B followed A, A caused B.
trigger_phrases: because of the resolution, the situation
